vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1365

plan.title: swarm: spawn_subagent review-cycle sub-agents can go completely dark with no visibility or monitoring from the parent

narratives.Description: swarm: spawn_subagent review-cycle sub-agents can go completely dark with no visibility or monitoring from the parent. Long-running Grok Build sub-agents on the hybrid path emit no heartbeat or progress channel, so stalled agents are indistinguishable from healthy ones at the monitor level.
narratives.Overview:
## Problem

When using the Grok Build hybrid swarm path (`spawn_subagent` + isolated worktrees), long-running review-cycle sub-agents can become completely invisible and unresponsive from the monitor's perspective.

### Observed symptoms (from #1166 swarm)

- Launched three parallel review-cycle sub-agents (one per remaining PR) with the explicit goal of driving them to 5/5.
- One agent (#1360) completed successfully and reported back with full details.
- The other two (#1362 and #1363) produced **zero visible output**:
  - No new commits in their worktrees
  - No comments posted to the PRs
  - No progress reports sent back to the parent
  - No completion notifications

From the monitor's point of view, they were indistinguishable from dead/stalled agents. Multiple manual checks (worktree git state + PR comments) showed no activity.

This is a recurring pattern when leaving review-cycle sub-agents running autonomously in the Grok Build environment.

## Root Cause / Contributing Factors

- `spawn_subagent` in the current Grok Build setup is intentionally low-visibility (background processes in worktrees).
- There is no built-in heartbeat, progress channel, or status reporting mechanism that the monitor can reliably poll.
- The canonical review-cycle poller prompt expects agents to post updates, but if an agent gets stuck (analysis, waiting for Greptile, tool issues, context pressure, silent failure), it can go completely silent.
- The swarm skill's monitoring phase has very weak support for observing or recovering from silent sub-agents.
- The only signals available are side effects (commits or PR comments). If the agent produces neither, the parent has almost no diagnostic power.

This is significantly worse than the Warp `start_agent` experience, where tabs provide at least some live visibility.

## Impact

- Makes parallel review work (a common swarm pattern) fragile and hard to manage.
- Forces the monitor into constant manual polling of worktrees and PRs.
- Increases risk of "zombie" review agents that consume resources without the parent knowing.
- Makes it difficult to know when to intervene, re-spawn, or take over manually.

## Proposed Improvements

We should improve handling in one or more of these areas:

1. **Standardized sub-agent reporting contract**
   - Define a lightweight expected behavior for long-running sub-agents (e.g., periodic status comments, or a simple `.deft-scratch/subagent-status/` file the monitor can poll).
   - Update the canonical review-cycle / poller template and `templates/agent-prompt-preamble.md` to include this expectation.

2. **Monitor-side tooling / patterns**
   - Add helpers (possibly in the swarm skill or a new `scripts/subagent_monitor.py`) that can watch a set of sub-agents by their worktrees + parent dispatch context.
   - Surface "last heard from", worktree activity, and basic health.
   - Time-based escalation (e.g., "no activity in 30+ minutes → flag to monitor").

3. **Better integration in the swarm skill**
   - Phase 4 (Monitor) and Phase 6 (post-PR review) should have explicit guidance and primitives for tracking review-cycle sub-agents.
   - Clear recovery paths when agents go dark (re-spawn with context, manual takeover, etc.).

4. **Grok Build platform improvements (longer term)**
   - If possible, expose some minimal status / lifecycle signals from `spawn_subagent` dispatches back to the parent conversation (even just "still alive" heartbeats).

## References

- Recent occurrence during 1166 swarm (attempt to drive remaining PRs to 5/5 via parallel review sub-agents).
- Related prior work: #1342 (Grok Build hybrid swarm support), #1353 (Windows Grok Build run_terminal_command limitations), #727 (sub-agent role separation and canonical poller template).
- AGENTS.md sections on sub-agent hygiene (#954), monitoring, and the current spawn_subagent model.

This feels like a missing piece in making the hybrid swarm path robust for real review work.

**Labels suggestion**: swarm, grok-build, review-cycle, determinism, monitoring
narratives.Cluster: Wave 2 (visibility, depends on #1366 Wave 1) of the Grok Build harness fragility cluster. Sibling Wave 2: #1368 (monitor reliability, parallelizable). Wave 3 dependent: #1369 (cascade automation). Cross-ref #1353. Cluster tracked via the `cluster:grok-harness` tag across four vBRIEFs (no GitHub umbrella issue filed).
narratives.UserStory: As a swarm monitor on the Grok Build hybrid path, I want long-running spawn_subagent review-cycle agents to publish a periodic heartbeat the parent can observe, so that silent or stalled agents are detectable without resorting to manual worktree polling.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Define a .deft-scratch/subagent-status/<agent-id>.json heartbeat contract with schema { agent_id, parent_id, last_heartbeat_at, last_message, phase, terminal_state? }; document the schema in docs/subagent-heartbeat.md and cite it from the swarm skill.
2. Add scripts/subagent_monitor.py that walks .deft-scratch/subagent-status/, reports each sub-agent's last_heartbeat_at and phase, and flags any record older than a configurable threshold (default 30 minutes). Three-state exit (0 ok / 1 stale / 2 config error). Routes gh capture through the #1366 helper.
3. Update templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md and templates/agent-prompt-preamble.md to declare the heartbeat contract and require periodic writes (cadence: every 2-3 minutes minimum during the poll loop).
4. Update skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md Phase 4 (Monitor) and Phase 6 (Sub-Agent Role Separation) to call out heartbeat polling as the canonical 'is this agent alive?' check on the Grok Build hybrid path and cite scripts/subagent_monitor.py.
5. Add tests/cli/test_subagent_monitor.py covering empty scratch dir (exit 0), fresh heartbeat under threshold (exit 0), stale heartbeat over threshold (exit 1), and malformed JSON (exit 1 with warning).
6. Add CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry referencing #1365 and the #1366 dependency.
narratives.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1365 and the #1166 swarm session where #1362 and #1363 went silent. Depends on #1366 safe-subprocess helper.

narrative.Acceptance: When an agent reads docs/subagent-heartbeat.md, the file shows the heartbeat contract documenting agent_id, parent_id, last_heartbeat_at, last_message, phase, and optional terminal_state, and the swarm skill and poller template both reference this doc.
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1365 -- Heartbeat schema and docs/subagent-heartbeat.md.

narrative.Acceptance: When invoked against a .deft-scratch/subagent-status/ directory, the helper emits a status report listing each agent's last_heartbeat_at and phase, exits 0 when every record is fresher than the threshold, exits 1 when any record is stale, and exits 2 when the scratch directory is missing or malformed.
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1365 -- scripts/subagent_monitor.py helper.

items.title: Poller and preamble templates require heartbeat writes

narrative.Acceptance: When a long-running review-cycle poller runs, templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md and templates/agent-prompt-preamble.md require the poller to emit a heartbeat write every 2-3 minutes to .deft-scratch/subagent-status/<agent-id>.json with the documented schema.
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1365 -- Poller and preamble templates require heartbeat writes.

items.title: Swarm skill cites heartbeat monitoring on Grok Build hybrid path

narrative.Acceptance: When the swarm skill Phase 4 or Phase 6 surfaces, it shows heartbeat polling as the canonical alive-check for spawn_subagent / Grok Build review-cycle sub-agents and references scripts/subagent_monitor.py as the helper.
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1365 -- Swarm skill cites heartbeat monitoring on Grok Build hybrid path.

items.title: Tests cover fresh, stale, and malformed cases

narrative.Acceptance: When pytest runs tests/cli/test_subagent_monitor.py, it validates an empty scratch directory, a fresh heartbeat under threshold, a stale heartbeat over threshold, and a malformed JSON record, and each case emits the documented exit code (0 ok, 1 stale, 2 config error).
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1365 -- Tests cover fresh, stale, and malformed cases.

references.title: Issue #1365: swarm: spawn_subagent review-cycle sub-agents can go completely dark with no visibility or monitoring from the parent

expected_outputs: scripts/subagent_monitor.py reports fresh / stale / malformed states with three-state exit
swarm SKILL.md Phase 4 and Phase 6 cite heartbeat polling and subagent_monitor.py
